candidates.evidence_quote: we had $346.7 million in available unused borrowing capacity under our revolving credit facility, net of letters of credit of $18.3 million

A violation of these covenants could negatively impact our liquidity by restricting our ability to borrow under the revolving credit facility and cause an acceleration of the amounts due under the credit facilities
the Noteholders agreed to exchange $83.6 million in aggregate principal amount of our outstanding 2025 Notes for approximately 7.5 million shares of our common stock and $3.3 million in cash

we entered into the Early Termination Agreements with the Derivative Counterparties. Upon settlement, we received approximately $118.2 million in cash and 0.3 million shares of common stock from the Derivative Counterparties
We estimate that our capital expenditures will total approximately $270 million to $290 million in 2024
which we use for operating expenses, development of new restaurants, remodeling or relocating older restaurants, investments in technology, dividend payments and share repurchases
our Board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on May 31, 2024
our Board canceled the remaining $57.5 million under our former share repurchase authorization and approved a new $350.0 million share repurchase authorization
we entered into the ASR Agreement, in connection with our previously announced 2024 Share Repurchase Program, with Wells Fargo to repurchase $220.0 million of our common stock

The decrease in net cash provided by operating activities during the thirteen weeks ended March 31, 2024 as compared to the thirteen weeks ended March 26, 2023 was primarily due to changes in working capital and lower net earnings
We have, and in the future may continue to have, negative working capital balances (as is common for many restaurant companies)
ongoing cash flows from restaurant operations and gift card sales are typically used to service debt obligations and to make capital expenditures
The amount of actual capital expenditures may be affected by general economic, financial, competitive, legislative and regulatory factors, among other things, including raw material constraints
Effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents

notes: Chunk contains a single event: 10-Q accession 0001546417-24-000113 (part 2 of 2), MD&A liquidity/capital resources continuation plus a Risk Factors stub; no fiscal_kpis in this chunk (those appear only on chunk_001).

Skipped (5): 'lower net earnings' (outcome metric inside cash-flow explanation, not a distinct cause here); 'investments in technology' (boilerplate cash-use list item, no specifics); current operating lease liabilities (descriptive balance composition, no causal claim); Risk Factors section (explicit no-material-changes stub, no content); general liquidity-adequacy assurance language (vague boilerplate).
All candidates come from one source document, so source_id/date are identical across candidates; dedup performed within chunk only.

capital_expenditure_guidance follows the {metric}_guidance convention for the forward 2024 capex estimate; raw_material_supply names the causal variable behind 'raw material constraints' (constraint = state).
